AskUserQuestion reserved for high-stakes + ambiguous + non-inferable. | Keep brainstorming interactive (rejected: contradicts the autonomy instruction) |
| 2026-05-21 | `yolo-ship` is a **project skill** (`.claude/skills/`, committed) and **`done = green review-clean PR, NOT merged`** | It wires into this repo's CodeRabbit + TODO.md + `.claude/memory/` conventions, so it belongs with the other day-1 project skills, not `~/.claude/skills`. Stop condition mirrors the user's spec exactly ("if everything looks good… you are done") — no auto-merge. | Personal skill in `~/.claude/skills` (rejected: CodeRabbit/TODO/memory wiring is repo-specific); auto-merge on green (rejected: user said stop at done) |
| 2026-05-21 | UI-bugs fix: implement the 5 small edits **inline** rather than via per-task subagents | yolo-ship mandates subagent-driven impl to protect orchestrator context, but the rationale is heavy exploration tasks. I already did the deep reads (root causes confirmed; ~5 surgical files). Spawning subagents to re-derive known context would cost MORE total tokens and risk divergence. Context budget stays well under the 300–400K cap. | Strict one-subagent-per-task (rejected here: overhead/divergence risk for tiny, fully-specified diffs) |
| 2026-05-21 | Title bug: replace the one-shot `assistantTurnCount !== 1` gate with a **bounded retry** (`<= MAX_TITLE_ATTEMPT_TURNS`, =3) while untitled | Root cause of "inconsistent" titles is the single attempt: if it's skipped/fails for ANY transient reason (jsonl-read race in `conversations:get`, llm:call error, validation rejection) the chat is permanently untitled. `title !== null` guard + `ifNull:true` on set-title already make re-attempts idempotent, so retrying on the next few assistant turns is safe; the cap bounds title-LLM spend if a model keeps emitting unusable titles. | Keep one-shot + only fix the UI (rejected: leaves "never generated" class unfixed); unbounded retry (rejected: unbounded LLM spend on degenerate output) |
| 2026-05-21 | Title bug (UI half): widen `generateTitle` poll window (more attempts + backoff, ~12-15s) instead of a client-side first-message fallback title | The 3×1s poll often gives up before the backend round-trip (jsonl read + haiku) lands, permanently caching "New Chat" until a full reload. Widening catches the common case. A client fallback title (first-message snippet) was rejected: it would later flip to the LLM title on reload — jarring. Residual (title landing after the widened window still needs a list() refresh) tracked as TODO. | Client-side fallback title (rejected: diverges from backend, flips on reload); push/websocket title update (rejected: out of scope for MVP) |
| 2026-05-21 | Attachment chip bug: **reconstruct the `attachment` block from the stored text-mention on the host read path** (`conversations:get`), gated to the conversation's own `.ax/uploads/<conversationId>/` prefix | Under runner-owned-sessions the jsonl stores the runner's *translation* (text-mention `User attached '<name>' at <path> (<mime>)`), not the original `attachment` block — so the original is genuinely gone. The mention embeds path+name+mime (everything the chip needs; `sizeBytes` is dropped by the render path anyway). Reconstruction needs no schema/table and **retroactively fixes already-broken chats** (no migration). Format owned by a shared `formatAttachmentMention`/`parseAttachmentMention` pair in `@ax/ipc-protocol` so producer (runner) and consumer (conversations) can't drift. Path-prefix gate + `AttachmentBlockSchema.safeParse` + role==='user'-only keep untrusted jsonl from injecting an out-of-scope downloadable ref. | Host-side per-turn attachment-block table overlaid on read (rejected: new table+hook+turnId-matching, heavier, and needs a migration to fix old chats; `sizeBytes` is the only extra fidelity and it's unused by the render path); client-side parse in blocksToParts (rejected: leaves the wire shape lossy for all other consumers of conversations:get) |
| 2026-05-21 | `@ax/web-tools` impl: branch the worktree off `feat/web-tools` HEAD (not main); PR base = `main`, carrying the design-spec + impl-plan doc commits into the PR | The two doc commits (497fb157, 450153bb) are part of this feature and belong in the same PR. Branching off main would orphan them. | Open PR against feat/web-tools (rejected: CodeRabbit only reviews PRs based on main); separate docs PR (rejected: needless split) |
| 2026-05-21 | Verified all 14 load-bearing plan claims via one Explore agent before implementing — grounding TRUE (core exports, `PluginError` code union incl. `invalid-payload`/`init-failed`, host-exec contract = full ToolCall + `{output}` wrap, `^0.91.1`, preset insertion points) | Plan itself flagged the `memory-search` `input.query` bug; don't trust signatures blindly. Item-14 Explore self-contradiction ("HookBus unwraps the payload") is WRONG — item 6 is ground truth: web-tools reads `call.input`, mirroring mcp-client, NOT memory-search. | Trust the plan as-written (rejected: cheap to verify; a wrong signature would fail a subagent mid-chain) |
| 2026-05-21 | Implement via ~6 **sequential** subagents grouped by coherent file-unit (scaffold / url-guard+anthropic-client / two tool executors / plugin+exports / canary / preset-wiring), not strict 1-per-12-tasks | Plan gives complete code per step; grouping keeps TDD checkpoints + the plan's per-step commits intact while cutting orchestration round-trips. Subagents share the worktree fs, so they MUST run sequentially (each builds on prior commits), never parallel. | Strict 1-subagent-per-task (rejected: 12 round-trips for a paint-by-numbers plan); inline (rejected: blows orchestrator context budget) |
| 2026-05-21 | `web_extract` output is `{ url, title?, text }` — drop the `truncated` field the design spec listed | Impl plan (Task 4 + Task 12 Step 3) resolves the spec/plan discrepancy by dropping `truncated` and syncing the spec: `max_content_tokens` already caps content server-side and a bare boolean adds no actionable signal for the agent. | Keep `truncated` (rejected: no consumer; would require tracking server-side clipping we don't observe) |
| 2026-05-21 | Scope the web-tools PR to web-tools only: rebase `feat/web-tools-impl --onto origin/main`, dropping the 2 unrelated commits `b3c87929` (auth-better baseURL) + `494b9e7c` (channel-web pill spacing) that rode on `feat/web-tools` | Reviewer-disciplined / scoped-PR convention (Vinay's documented style): a "web-tools" PR shouldn't carry unrelated fixes — they'd muddy CodeRabbit's review + the PR narrative. Branch lacked only PR #120 with zero file overlap, so the rebase is conflict-free. The 2 dropped commits are PRESERVED on the untouched original `feat/web-tools` branch; flagged in TODO + final report so the user can ship them separately. | Keep them in the PR (rejected: scope creep, unrelated review surface); ask the user (rejected: inferable from convention; commits preserved either way) |
| 2026-05-21 | Live-title-SSE: worktree branched **fresh from origin/main** (drops the 2 unpushed local commits f1e2deb7 channel-web spacing + c168fb8c auth baseURL — same pair as entry 27, recommitted) for a clean PR diff | Same reviewer-disciplined scoped-PR convention as entry 27; those 2 fixes don't intersect the title-SSE files. They stay on local `main` for the user to push separately. | Branch from local HEAD (rejected: PR diff would carry the 2 unrelated fixes) |
| 2026-05-21 | Live-title-SSE design (user-approved + autonomous): **per-user** route `GET /api/chat/title-events` (one connection covers all sidebar rows); fire `conversations:title-updated` from the **set-title write point** (one source of truth, covers future rename UI); client **updates the row in place** via new `session-store.applyTitle` + **bumpVersion()-on-connect resync** (no server replay buffer); mount the consumer in **AppContent** not Sidebar (Sidebar unmounts when admin panel opens). Additive — existing client poll untouched. Plan docs allowlisted in `.gitignore` (project default ignores `docs/plans/*`). | Per-user matches the sidebar's need; set-title is the only title writer; direct-update avoids a refetch per frame while onOpen-resync covers disconnect gaps; AppContent is the stable post-auth shell. In-process bus (single-replica) matches the chat-SSE precedent — cross-replica deferred (design I6). | Per-conversation SSE (rejected: N connections); fire from conversation-titles (rejected: not the write point); refetch-per-frame (rejected: wasteful); replace the poll (rejected: user chose additive) |

1	---
2	name: project_web_tools_pr121
3	description: "@ax/web-tools (host-executed web_search + web_extract) shipped as PR #121 — green + review-clean, not merged"
4	metadata: 
5	  node_type: memory
6	  type: project
7	  originSessionId: bb27b71d-61e8-46f1-a42a-570a69766fca
8	---
9	
10	`@ax/web-tools` opened as **PR #121** (https://github.com/project-ax/ax-next/pull/121), base `main`, branch `feat/web-tools-impl`. Built end-to-end via `/yolo-ship` from the committed plan `docs/plans/2026-05-21-web-tools-impl.md`. **Stopped at green + review-clean per yolo-ship — NOT merged.**
11	
12	**What it is:** two `executesIn:'host'` tools available to every agent — `web_search` and `web_extract` — backed by Anthropic's *server-side* `web_search_20250305` / `web_fetch_20250910`. The host executor makes its own `@anthropic-ai/sdk` Messages call (`max_uses:1`); Anthropic does all egress, so the sandbox egress lock is untouched and the host's only outbound is `api.anthropic.com` (same as `@ax/llm-anthropic`). Output is backend-neutral (`encrypted_content` stripped). Gated on the global `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY`; `createWebToolsPlugin({enabled:false})` is the kill-switch. Loaded in **both** CLI + k8s presets same PR (half-wired window CLOSED) with a real-tool-dispatcher canary.
13	
14	**Build shape:** 21 commits (2 doc + 12 plan tasks via 6 sequential subagents + 1 TODO + 6 CodeRabbit fixes). 29 package tests. The plan's host-exec contract claim held: `tool:execute:<name>` gets the full `ToolCall`, executor reads `call.input`, IPC wraps return in `{output}` — mirrors `@ax/mcp-client`, NOT `memory-search` (whose bare-`input` read is a real bug, now tracked in TODO.md).
15	
16	**Plan gap I caught:** the plan omitted the tsc **project reference** for the new package in `packages/cli/tsconfig.json` and `presets/k8s/tsconfig.json` — without it `pnpm build` can't resolve `@ax/web-tools`. The repo lists every workspace plugin dep as a `references` entry; added it both places.
17	
18	**CodeRabbit (10 actionable + 1 nitpick, all valid quick wins, all fixed + resolved):** fail-fast on pause-cap exhaustion + missing `web_search_tool_result` block (were silent empty-success); private/link-local/ULA IPv6 in url-guard (only `::1` was caught); init-time config validation (trim apiKey, bounds-check timeoutMs/maxContentTokens); redact caller URL from web_extract error msg; canary stub fail-fast + structural `encrypted_content` assert; design-spec SSRF wording. Confirmed CodeRabbit auto-review *does* re-review each push (it resolved all 10 threads after the fix push) — the manual `@coderabbitai review` I sent was redundant; matches [[project_coderabbit_review_constraints]].
19	
20	**Deferred (TODO.md):** fix `memory-search` `call.input` bug; real-API MANUAL-ACCEPTANCE walk (needs Console web-search enablement); two unrelated commits parked on `feat/web-tools` (`b3c87929` auth-better baseURL, `494b9e7c` channel-web spacing) — dropped via rebase to scope the PR, preserved on `feat/web-tools`; cost-metering YAGNI.
21

<system-reminder>This memory is 4 days old. Memories are point-in-time observations, not live state — claims about code behavior or file:line citations may be outdated. Verify against current code before asserting as fact.</system-reminder>
1	---
2	name: project-credential-egress-via-skills
3	description: "Design direction for how agents gain access to new credentialed hosts — skills (or admin agent record), not in-session approval gates"
4	metadata: 
5	  node_type: memory
6	  type: project
7	  originSessionId: 156d1273-18d1-4285-b586-fdb68cdd1a6c
8	---
9	
10	Design direction reached 2026-05-17 via brainstorm: **credentialed host access flows through skill installation (or the admin-configured agent record), not through an in-session approval gate**. Uncredentialed GETs *may* use a lightweight in-session approval gate as a separate tier — the worst case there is leaking the URL the model chose, not credential theft.
11	
12	**Why:** Skills bundle `allowedHosts` + `requiredCredentials` into one auditable artifact authored once and reused across users/agents. Aligns with invariant #5 (capabilities explicit and minimized). Dodges the env-snapshotting problem — the runner snapshots env at `proxy:open-session` and the SDK doesn't re-read it, so adding a placeholder mid-session has nothing to substitute against; `proxy:rotate-session` deliberately preserves existing placeholders for the same reason. Installing a skill → respawn agent is the expected mental model, not a regression of a hot-path. Also kills the prompt-injection surface where the model could social-engineer a one-time mid-chat approval.
13	
14	**How to apply:** When someone proposes a "mid-session approve new host + attach credential" UX, push back toward the skills path for the credentialed case. The in-session gate is acceptable ONLY for uncredentialed GET-style access, and only after the tier split is explicit in the design. When designing the credential-egress UX, the moment a credential is involved, the artifact has to be a skill (or an admin-edited agent record), reviewed out-of-band.
15	
16	**Status:** Direction agreed, design doc drafted 2026-05-17 at `docs/plans/2026-05-17-skill-install-workflow-design.md`. Not yet planned/built.
17	
18	**Critical SDK-contract findings folded into the design (caught during review):**
19	
20	1. `@anthropic-ai/claude-agent-sdk@0.2.119` discovers skills from two roots: `<cwd>/.claude/skills/*/SKILL.md` (`'project'` source) and `<user-config-root>/skills/*/SKILL.md` (`'user'` source). The user-config root is `process.env.CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR ?? path.join(os.homedir(), '.claude')` — verified in installed `sdk.mjs` line 16.
21	2. Today's runner does NOT pass `settingSources` or include `'Skill'` in `allowedTools` — the SDK currently sees ZERO skills. The validator-skill package validates a convention the SDK never loaded. Phase 0 of the impl plan: wire `settingSources: ['user', 'project']` + `'Skill'` in `allowedTools` in `agent-claude-sdk-runner/src/main.ts`.
22	3. **NO workspace-skill migration needed.** Keep `.ax/skills/` as the git-tracked workspace path. Sandbox creates a symlink `<workspace>/.claude/skills` → `<workspace>/.ax/skills` at session-open so the SDK's hardcoded `.claude/skills/` discovery works through it. For host-installed (user-source) skills, set `CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR=$HOME/.ax/session` and materialize under `$HOME/.ax/session/skills/<id>/SKILL.md`. Net result: `.ax/` stays the only user-visible namespace; SDK contract satisfied via env-var redirect + symlink, no rename.
23	
24	v2 has the `validator-skill` package (shape validation) but no user-facing "install third-party skill" workflow — that's the missing piece. Related invariants: I1 (real creds never enter the sandbox), I5 (capabilities explicit), I6 (one UI design language — the skill-install UI lives in channel-web).
25	
26	Related: [[project_phase_1a_shipped]] (credential-proxy + bridge mechanics), [[feedback_no_oauth_credentials]] (API-key-only constraint that simplifies the skill credential schema).
27

file.content:
---
name: project-credential-egress-via-skills
description: "Design direction for how agents gain access to new credentialed hosts — skills (or admin agent record), not in-session approval gates"
metadata: 
  node_type: memory
  type: project
  originSessionId: 156d1273-18d1-4285-b586-fdb68cdd1a6c
---

Design direction reached 2026-05-17 via brainstorm: **credentialed host access flows through skill installation (or the admin-configured agent record), not through an in-session approval gate**. Uncredentialed GETs *may* use a lightweight in-session approval gate as a separate tier — the worst case there is leaking the URL the model chose, not credential theft.

**Why:** Skills bundle `allowedHosts` + `requiredCredentials` into one auditable artifact authored once and reused across users/agents. Aligns with invariant #5 (capabilities explicit and minimized). Dodges the env-snapshotting problem — the runner snapshots env at `proxy:open-session` and the SDK doesn't re-read it, so adding a placeholder mid-session has nothing to substitute against; `proxy:rotate-session` deliberately preserves existing placeholders for the same reason. Installing a skill → respawn agent is the expected mental model, not a regression of a hot-path. Also kills the prompt-injection surface where the model could social-engineer a one-time mid-chat approval.

**How to apply:** When someone proposes a "mid-session approve new host + attach credential" UX, push back toward the skills path for the credentialed case. The in-session gate is acceptable ONLY for uncredentialed GET-style access, and only after the tier split is explicit in the design. When designing the credential-egress UX, the moment a credential is involved, the artifact has to be a skill (or an admin-edited agent record), reviewed out-of-band.

**Status:** Direction agreed, design doc drafted 2026-05-17 at `docs/plans/2026-05-17-skill-install-workflow-design.md`. Not yet planned/built.

**Critical SDK-contract findings folded into the design (caught during review):**

1. `@anthropic-ai/claude-agent-sdk@0.2.119` discovers skills from two roots: `<cwd>/.claude/skills/*/SKILL.md` (`'project'` source) and `<user-config-root>/skills/*/SKILL.md` (`'user'` source). The user-config root is `process.env.CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR ?? path.join(os.homedir(), '.claude')` — verified in installed `sdk.mjs` line 16.
2. Today's runner does NOT pass `settingSources` or include `'Skill'` in `allowedTools` — the SDK currently sees ZERO skills. The validator-skill package validates a convention the SDK never loaded. Phase 0 of the impl plan: wire `settingSources: ['user', 'project']` + `'Skill'` in `allowedTools` in `agent-claude-sdk-runner/src/main.ts`.
3. **NO workspace-skill migration needed.** Keep `.ax/skills/` as the git-tracked workspace path. Sandbox creates a symlink `<workspace>/.claude/skills` → `<workspace>/.ax/skills` at session-open so the SDK's hardcoded `.claude/skills/` discovery works through it. For host-installed (user-source) skills, set `CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR=$HOME/.ax/session` and materialize under `$HOME/.ax/session/skills/<id>/SKILL.md`. Net result: `.ax/` stays the only user-visible namespace; SDK contract satisfied via env-var redirect + symlink, no rename.

v2 has the `validator-skill` package (shape validation) but no user-facing "install third-party skill" workflow — that's the missing piece. Related invariants: I1 (real creds never enter the sandbox), I5 (capabilities explicit), I6 (one UI design language — the skill-install UI lives in channel-web).

Related: [[project_phase_1a_shipped]] (credential-proxy + bridge mechanics), [[feedback_no_oauth_credentials]] (API-key-only constraint that simplifies the skill credential schema).

30	  + sandbox-subprocess materialize tests.

42	  `{ skillId }` / output `{ available, currentVersion, latestVersion?,
43	  latestSkillMd? }`); alternate-impl friendly per boundary review.

62	The sandbox-side zod schemas (`McpServerSchema` in both sandbox-k8s and

---
name: skills-capability-lifecycle-phases-bc
description: Phase 1 follow-ups B (MCP-skill bundling) and C (sourceUrl + refresh) shipped as PRs
metadata: 
  node_type: memory
  type: project
  originSessionId: a74c437d-3ccb-41ed-95a1-40c5f18ef0c9
---

2026-05-20 — closed two of the three Phase 1 follow-ups from PR #96 (the
capability + lifecycle pair). Phase A (system-prompt fold for non-SDK
runners) stays parked behind its trigger.

**Phase B — MCP-skill bundling (PR #113 MERGED 2026-05-21 as `b9bab96b`)**,
formerly branch `worktree-skills-mcp-bundling-phase-b` (branch deleted post-merge):

- Manifest grammar gains `capabilities.mcpServers[]` (stdio + http, command
  allowlist `npx|node|bun|uvx|python|python3`, args ≤32 × 256, http URL
  https-only with HOSTNAME_RE host folded into per-server `allowedHosts`).
- New error codes `invalid-mcp-command`, `invalid-mcp-transport`.
- Orchestrator unions per-skill `mcpServers` into the sandbox payload; both
  sandbox impls (k8s + subprocess) carry structural `McpServerSchema`
  copies and re-validate via zod (invariant I2).
- SDK runner + subprocess sandbox materialize per-skill `.mcp.json`
  (mode 0o444) next to `SKILL.md` so the Claude SDK auto-discovers.
- Security-checklist note saved at
  `docs/plans/2026-05-20-skills-mcp-bundling-security-note.md`.
- 6 commits; +5 manifest tests + 1 store + 1 admin-route + orchestrator
  union test + sdk-runner .mcp.json tests + sandbox-k8s schema rejection
  + sandbox-subprocess materialize tests.

**Phase C — sourceUrl + version-aware refresh (PR #114 MERGED 2026-05-21 as `dbe07d3c`)**,
formerly stacked on Phase B (rebased onto main after #113 merge):

- `skills_v1_skills.source_url TEXT NULL` (additive migration).
- `SkillSummary`/`SkillDetail` gain optional `sourceUrl`. ResolvedSkill
  intentionally does NOT — resolve is the per-session capability surface,
  has no business knowing the provenance.
- Manifest grammar parses optional top-level `sourceUrl:` (https-only,
  HOSTNAME_RE host).
- New service hook `skills:check-for-updates` (storage-agnostic input
  `{ skillId }` / output `{ available, currentVersion, latestVersion?,
  latestSkillMd? }`); alternate-impl friendly per boundary review.
- New admin routes `POST /admin/skills/:id/check-update` and
  `POST /admin/skills/:id/refresh-from-source`.
- SkillsTab fires per-skill check-update on mount for skills with
  `sourceUrl`; per-skill failures swallowed silently. Badge + Update
  button surface when `available === true`.
- C-design.1 LOCKED: latest-wins, no per-attachment pinned version (YAGNI
  until multi-tenant scope axis lands).
- C-design.2 LOCKED: HTTPS only, no IP literals, no bare-host.
- 5 commits; tests 86 → 98 (skills) + 454 → 458 (channel-web).

**Why:** Both follow-ups had been deferred at PR #96 close; they're
non-half-wired-window candidates because each closes its surface
end-to-end in a single PR. See [[feedback_half_wired_window_pattern.md]].

**How to apply:** When investigating skills capability bugs or extending
the manifest grammar in the future, the parser at
`packages/skills/src/manifest.ts` is now the source of truth for
`capabilities.mcpServers` (Phase B) AND top-level `sourceUrl` (Phase C).
The sandbox-side zod schemas (`McpServerSchema` in both sandbox-k8s and
sandbox-subprocess) are the trust-boundary re-validation. When/if Phase A
fires, it needs to split `InstalledSkillForSandbox.skillMd` into
`{ id, description, bodyMd, manifestYaml }` end-to-end — the
orchestrator's per-skill payload shape is the place to start.

**Out of scope (Plan 2):**
- User-installable / scope axis (Phase D).
- Workspace-promote (Phase E).
- E2e canary infra (Phase F).
- Phase A's actual fold — wait for `agent-native-runner` or a
  `test-harness` LLM-calling code path.

19	```

file.content:
---
name: docker-build-cache-runner-fixes
description: "When verifying a runner-code fix in the kind cluster, force a fresh docker build or grep the compiled output inside the image before testing — incremental builds can silently reuse a pre-fix layer."
metadata: 
  node_type: memory
  type: feedback
  originSessionId: 72f92c44-7a1e-4afa-b923-f91812ea856a
---

When the fix you're verifying in `ax-next-dev` is **runner-code** (anything in `packages/agent-claude-sdk-runner/`), don't trust an incremental `docker build`. The image's compiled `main.js` can silently retain a pre-fix copy even though you're building from `main` with the fix merged.

**Why:** Docker layer caching keyed on filesystem state can decide the runner-bake layer is "the same" and reuse it. The host's grep of source on disk passes, but the bundled output the runner pod actually executes is the stale one.

**How to apply:** Before drawing conclusions from "but the fix is in!", verify the compiled output inside the image:

```bash
docker run --rm ax-next/agent:dev grep -A8 "event('event.turn-end'" \
  /opt/ax-next/host/node_modules/.pnpm/@ax+agent-claude-sdk-runner@file+packages+agent-claude-sdk-runner/node_modules/@ax/agent-claude-sdk-runner/dist/main.js
```

If the fix isn't there, `docker build --no-cache -t ax-next/agent:dev -f container/agent/Dockerfile .` and retry. Adds ~2 min to the build but saves the ~5 min round-trip of "deploy → curl → wait → confirm nothing changed → realize the image is stale → rebuild".

Host-only (non-runner) fixes ride the fast loop's hostPath mount — see `[[k8s-acceptance-loop]]` — and don't hit this problem.

input.prompt:
You are verifying structural claims in a design doc against the CURRENT code, so an implementation plan can be written. Work in the worktree: `/Users/vpulim/dev/ai/ax-next/.claude/worktrees/credentialed-cli-and-git-auth`. This is the ax-next monorepo (pnpm workspaces, packages under `packages/`). Read excerpts, report CONCLUSIONS with exact `file:line` citations and short code shapes — do NOT dump whole files.

Sub-project B = "git Basic-auth via Basic-aware substitution in the credential-proxy MITM path". Answer ALL of these precisely:

1. **credential-proxy listener** — In `packages/credential-proxy/src/listener.ts`: how does the MITM request path currently work? Specifically: (a) where/how does it do CONNECT handling and TLS interception? (b) where does it currently apply credential substitution to client→upstream bytes — is it per-chunk on the decrypted stream, or buffered? Cite the function name and line range. (c) Is there any request-head buffering today, or is it pure streaming? (d) where does the canary scan run on the request bytes (function + line)?

2. **registry substitution** — In `packages/credential-proxy/src/registry.ts`: what is the exact signature and behavior of the substitution function (the design calls it `registry.replaceAll`)? Does that name exist, or is it called something else? Does it operate on a Buffer or string? Is there a canary-scan function exposed (or where does canary scanning live)? Cite signatures + lines.

3. **placeholder shape** — Confirm the placeholder format `ax-cred:<hex>`. Where is it generated and where does the registry map placeholder→real-secret? Cite.

You are verifying structural claims in a design doc against the CURRENT code, so an implementation plan can be written. Work in the worktree: `/Users/vpulim/dev/ai/ax-next/.claude/worktrees/credentialed-cli-and-git-auth`. This is the ax-next monorepo (pnpm workspaces, packages under `packages/`). Sub-project D = "CLI tool provisioning: a `capabilities.packages` manifest grammar (npm/pypi name-only), orchestrator auto-allowlist of registry hosts, and Dockerfile additions (uv/uvx + python3)". Answer ALL of these precisely:

content.text:
Now I have all the information needed. Let me compile the full findings.

---

**1. credential-proxy listener — MITM request path**

**File:** `/Users/vpulim/dev/ai/ax-next/.claude/worktrees/credentialed-cli-and-git-auth/packages/credential-proxy/src/listener.ts`

**(a) CONNECT handling and TLS interception:**
- `handleCONNECT` (lines 608–759) receives every HTTP `CONNECT` request via `server.on('connect', handleCONNECT)` (line 764).
- After allowlist + DNS checks, if the host is NOT in any session's `bypassMITM`, it delegates to `handleMITMConnect` (lines 438–590).
- `handleMITMConnect` calls `generateDomainCert(hostname, ca)`, writes `HTTP/1.1 200 Connection Established\r\n\r\n` to the client socket, wraps the client socket in `new tls.TLSSocket(clientSocket, { isServer: true, ... })` (lines 459–463), and opens the upstream with `tls.connect({ host: resolvedIP, ..., ca: [...tls.rootCertificates, ca.cert] })` (lines 470–475). This is the full MITM TLS termination.

**(b) Credential substitution — per-chunk, not buffered:**
- Substitution is per-chunk on the `clientTls 'data'` event (lines 508–538). There is no request head buffering for parsing — each decoded TLS chunk is immediately passed through `registry.replaceAllBuffer(chunk)` and written to `targetTls`.
- The `head` buffer (bytes from before the upstream socket existed) is also substituted and flushed separately at lines 553–557.

**(c) Request-head buffering:**
- There is NO HTTP-level request-head buffering. The `head: Buffer` parameter coming into `handleMITMConnect` is the raw bytes the server passed with the CONNECT upgrade event (often empty or containing early TLS handshake bytes). It is flushed via `registry.replaceAllBuffer(head)` at line 554 if non-empty. There is no `\r\n\r\n` parsing, no accumulation of a full HTTP request before forwarding.

**(d) Canary scan — function and location:**
- There is no separate canary-scanner function. The scan is inline in `handleMITMConnect`'s `clientTls 'data'` listener (lines 515–533): `collectCanaryTokens(sessions)` gathers tokens (lines 217–223), and for each chunk the code does `chunk.includes(token)` (line 516). The canary scan runs on every raw decrypted chunk, before substitution.
- `collectCanaryTokens` is at lines 217–223; it is called once per connection at line 505.

---

**2. registry substitution**

**File:** `/Users/vpulim/dev/ai/ax-next/.claude/worktrees/credentialed-cli-and-git-auth/packages/credential-proxy/src/registry.ts`

Both `CredentialPlaceholderMap` and `SharedCredentialRegistry` expose the function.

**`CredentialPlaceholderMap.replaceAll`** (lines 62–69): signature `replaceAll(input: string): string` — operates on a string, uses `split(ph).join(real)` for each placeholder.

**`SharedCredentialRegistry.replaceAll`** (lines 129–135): signature `replaceAll(input: string): string` — iterates each session's map and chains `map.replaceAll(result)`.

**`SharedCredentialRegistry.replaceAllBuffer`** (lines 139–143): signature `replaceAllBuffer(input: Buffer): Buffer` — converts to UTF-8 string, short-circuits via `hasPlaceholders`, returns original `Buffer` by identity if no placeholders present.

The design's name `registry.replaceAll` **does exist** on `SharedCredentialRegistry` (line 129). The listener calls `registry.replaceAllBuffer` (line 535), not `replaceAll` directly.

There is **no separate canary-scan function** in registry.ts. Canary scanning lives entirely inline in `listener.ts` using `chunk.includes(token)` (line 516). The `hasPlaceholders` method on both classes is a placeholder-presence check, not a canary scanner.

---

**3. Placeholder shape**

Confirmed: `ax-cred:<hex>` — specifically `ax-cred:<32 lowercase hex chars>` (16 random bytes encoded).

**Generated at:** `registry.ts:29` — `const placeholder = \`ax-cred:${randomBytes(16).toString('hex')}\``

**Map stored at:** `registry.ts:16–18` — `CredentialPlaceholderMap` holds two private Maps: `placeholderToReal` (placeholder → real value) and `nameToPlaceholder` (env var name → placeholder). `SharedCredentialRegistry` aggregates per-session `CredentialPlaceholderMap` instances (line 99).

The regex in `proxy-startup.ts:105` — `const PLACEHOLDER_RE = /^ax-cred:[0-9a-f]{32}$/` — validates this shape at the consumer end, confirming 32 hex chars (16 bytes).

---

**4. proxy-startup forwarding — placeholder value-shape**

**File:** `/Users/vpulim/dev/ai/ax-next/.claude/worktrees/credentialed-cli-and-git-auth/packages/agent-claude-sdk-runner/src/proxy-startup.ts`

Lines 198–209: The code iterates `process.env` and, for each key, if `isEnvAllowed(k)` passes it through, and **otherwise** if `PLACEHOLDER_RE.test(v)` (i.e., the value matches `^ax-cred:[0-9a-f]{32}$`) it also forwards it. This means ANY env var whose value is a credential placeholder is forwarded into `anthropicEnv`, regardless of whether its key is on the allowlist.

Lines 225–231: `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` is additionally asserted to hold a valid placeholder — `PLACEHOLDER_RE.test(placeholder)` must pass or a `MissingEnvError` is thrown, and then `anthropicEnv.ANTHROPIC_API_KEY = placeholder` is set explicitly.

The "value-shape" the design refers to is exactly `ax-cred:[0-9a-f]{32}` — forwarding is gated on that regex match, not on a key-name allowlist.

---

**5. Sandbox env GIT_ vars**

**(a) sandbox-k8s pod-spec:**
**File:** `/Users/vpulim/dev/ai/ax-next/.claude/worktrees/credentialed-cli-and-git-auth/packages/sandbox-k8s/src/pod-spec.ts`, lines 180–201.
The `gitParanoidEnv` array contains: `GIT_CONFIG_NOSYSTEM=1`, `GIT_CONFIG_GLOBAL=/dev/null`, `GIT_TERMINAL_PROMPT=0`, `GIT_AUTHOR_NAME`, `GIT_AUTHOR_EMAIL`, `GIT_COMMITTER_NAME`, `GIT_COMMITTER_EMAIL`, `GIT_CONFIG_COUNT=1`, `GIT_CONFIG_KEY_0=safe.directory`, `GIT_CONFIG_VALUE_0=*`.

**(b) sandbox-subprocess open-session:**
**File:** `/Users/vpulim/dev/ai/ax-next/.claude/worktrees/credentialed-cli-and-git-auth/packages/sandbox-subprocess/src/open-session.ts`, lines 495–504.
Identical set as an object literal (plain JS object, not an array): `GIT_CONFIG_NOSYSTEM`, `GIT_CONFIG_GLOBAL`, `GIT_TERMINAL_PROMPT=0`, `GIT_AUTHOR_NAME/EMAIL`, `GIT_COMMITTER_NAME/EMAIL`, `GIT_CONFIG_COUNT/KEY_0/VALUE_0`.

**`GIT_TERMINAL_PROMPT`** is already set in BOTH places (`value: '0'` / `'0'`).

**1. credential-proxy listener — MITM request path**

**File:** `/Users/vpulim/dev/ai/ax-next/.claude/worktrees/credentialed-cli-and-git-auth/packages/credential-proxy/src/listener.ts`

**(a) CONNECT handling and TLS interception:**
- `handleCONNECT` (lines 608–759) receives every HTTP `CONNECT` request via `server.on('connect', handleCONNECT)` (line 764).
- After allowlist + DNS checks, if the host is NOT in any session's `bypassMITM`, it delegates to `handleMITMConnect` (lines 438–590).
- `handleMITMConnect` calls `generateDomainCert(hostname, ca)`, writes `HTTP/1.1 200 Connection Established\r\n\r\n` to the client socket, wraps the client socket in `new tls.TLSSocket(clientSocket, { isServer: true, ... })` (lines 459–463), and opens the upstream with `tls.connect({ host: resolvedIP, ..., ca: [...tls.rootCertificates, ca.cert] })` (lines 470–475). This is the full MITM TLS termination.

**(b) Credential substitution — per-chunk, not buffered:**
- Substitution is per-chunk on the `clientTls 'data'` event (lines 508–538). There is no request head buffering for parsing — each decoded TLS chunk is immediately passed through `registry.replaceAllBuffer(chunk)` and written to `targetTls`.
- The `head` buffer (bytes from before the upstream socket existed) is also substituted and flushed separately at lines 553–557.

**(c) Request-head buffering:**
- There is NO HTTP-level request-head buffering. The `head: Buffer` parameter coming into `handleMITMConnect` is the raw bytes the server passed with the CONNECT upgrade event (often empty or containing early TLS handshake bytes). It is flushed via `registry.replaceAllBuffer(head)` at line 554 if non-empty. There is no `\r\n\r\n` parsing, no accumulation of a full HTTP request before forwarding.

**(d) Canary scan — function and location:**
- There is no separate canary-scanner function. The scan is inline in `handleMITMConnect`'s `clientTls 'data'` listener (lines 515–533): `collectCanaryTokens(sessions)` gathers tokens (lines 217–223), and for each chunk the code does `chunk.includes(token)` (line 516). The canary scan runs on every raw decrypted chunk, before substitution.

**`SharedCredentialRegistry.replaceAll`** (lines 129–135): signature `replaceAll(input: string): string` — iterates each session's map and chains `map.replaceAll(result)`.

**`SharedCredentialRegistry.replaceAllBuffer`** (lines 139–143): signature `replaceAllBuffer(input: Buffer): Buffer` — converts to UTF-8 string, short-circuits via `hasPlaceholders`, returns original `Buffer` by identity if no placeholders present.

The design's name `registry.replaceAll` **does exist** on `SharedCredentialRegistry` (line 129). The listener calls `registry.replaceAllBuffer` (line 535), not `replaceAll` directly.

There is **no separate canary-scan function** in registry.ts. Canary scanning lives entirely inline in `listener.ts` using `chunk.includes(token)` (line 516). The `hasPlaceholders` method on both classes is a placeholder-presence check, not a canary scanner.

---

**3. Placeholder shape**

Confirmed: `ax-cred:<hex>` — specifically `ax-cred:<32 lowercase hex chars>` (16 random bytes encoded).

**Generated at:** `registry.ts:29` — `const placeholder = \`ax-cred:${randomBytes(16).toString('hex')}\``

**Map stored at:** `registry.ts:16–18` — `CredentialPlaceholderMap` holds two private Maps: `placeholderToReal` (placeholder → real value) and `nameToPlaceholder` (env var name → placeholder). `SharedCredentialRegistry` aggregates per-session `CredentialPlaceholderMap` instances (line 99).

The regex in `proxy-startup.ts:105` — `const PLACEHOLDER_RE = /^ax-cred:[0-9a-f]{32}$/` — validates this shape at the consumer end, confirming 32 hex chars (16 bytes).

---

**4. proxy-startup forwarding — placeholder value-shape**

**File:** `/Users/vpulim/dev/ai/ax-next/.claude/worktrees/credentialed-cli-and-git-auth/packages/agent-claude-sdk-runner/src/proxy-startup.ts`

Lines 198–209: The code iterates `process.env` and, for each key, if `isEnvAllowed(k)` passes it through, and **otherwise** if `PLACEHOLDER_RE.test(v)` (i.e., the value matches `^ax-cred:[0-9a-f]{32}$`) it also forwards it. This means ANY env var whose value is a credential placeholder is forwarded into `anthropicEnv`, regardless of whether its key is on the allowlist.

Lines 225–231: `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` is additionally asserted to hold a valid placeholder — `PLACEHOLDER_RE.test(placeholder)` must pass or a `MissingEnvError` is thrown, and then `anthropicEnv.ANTHROPIC_API_KEY = placeholder` is set explicitly.

The "value-shape" the design refers to is exactly `ax-cred:[0-9a-f]{32}` — forwarding is gated on that regex match, not on a key-name allowlist.

I now have complete information for all 9 questions. Here is the report:

---

## Findings

### 1. Manifest/capabilities parser location

**BOTH packages exist today.** The design doc §5.8 is correct AND project memory is partially correct — they describe different layers:

- `@ax/skills-parser` (`packages/skills-parser/`) is the **actual parser package**. It contains `src/manifest.ts` (the `parseSkillManifest` function) and `src/capabilities.ts` (the type definitions). It has no dependencies on zod, only `js-yaml`.
- `@ax/skills` (`packages/skills/`) depends on `@ax/skills-parser` as a workspace dep. Its `packages/skills/src/manifest.ts` is a **one-line re-export**: `export { parseSkillManifest } from '@ax/skills-parser';`

The authoritative parser lives at:
- `/packages/skills-parser/src/manifest.ts` (`parseSkillManifest`)
- `/packages/skills-parser/src/capabilities.ts` (type definitions: `SkillCapabilities`, `McpServerSpec`, `CapabilitySlot`)

The `@ax/skills/src/manifest.ts` path referenced by project memory is a shim, not the implementation.

---

### 2. Capabilities grammar

**Validation: hand-rolled, no zod.** The parser is pure TypeScript with js-yaml. There is no zod in `@ax/skills-parser`.

**`SkillCapabilities` shape** (`packages/skills-parser/src/capabilities.ts:1-22`):
```ts
interface CapabilitySlot { slot: string; kind: 'api-key'; description?: string; }
interface McpServerSpec {
  name: string; transport: 'stdio' | 'http';
  command?: string; args?: string[]; env?: Record<string, string>; url?: string;
  allowedHosts: string[];      // union of per-server + url host
  credentials: CapabilitySlot[];
}
interface SkillCapabilities {
  allowedHosts: string[]; credentials: CapabilitySlot[]; mcpServers: McpServerSpec[];
}
```

**Key caps and validations** (`packages/skills-parser/src/manifest.ts`):
- `MCP_SERVERS_MAX = 8` (line 84), `MCP_ARGS_MAX = 32` (line 85), `MCP_ARG_LEN_MAX = 256` (line 86)
- `MCP_ENV_MAX = 32` (line 90), `MCP_ENV_LEN_MAX = 256` (line 91)
- Description max 240 chars (line 438)
- Inline-secret scan: `findSecretKey()` (line 41) walks all YAML nodes looking for keys in `SECRET_KEYS = new Set(['apiKey', 'token', 'password', 'secret'])` (line 31) — cyclic-safe via WeakSet
- Hosts: no wildcard, no scheme, no path, no IPv4, must match `HOSTNAME_RE` (two-label minimum)
- Slot names: `SLOT_RE = /^[A-Z][A-Z0-9_]{0,63}$/` (line 70); only `kind: 'api-key'` accepted; duplicate slots rejected with `duplicate-slot`
- Per-server hosts accumulated into parent `allowedHosts` set (the proxy sees the union)

---

### 3. MCP_COMMAND_ALLOW

Defined at `packages/skills-parser/src/manifest.ts:80`:
```ts
const MCP_COMMAND_ALLOW = new Set(['npx', 'node', 'bun', 'uvx', 'python', 'python3']);
```
Applied at line 262: any `stdio` command not in this set returns `{ ok: false, code: 'invalid-mcp-command', ... }`. Note: `uvx` and `python3` are already present — the design's proposed additions are already in the allowlist.

---

### 4. ResolvedSkill.capabilities and skills:resolve

`ResolvedSkill` is defined at `packages/skills/src/types.ts:31-36`:
```ts
export interface ResolvedSkill {
  id: string;
  capabilities: SkillCapabilities;   // imported from @ax/skills-parser
  bodyMd: string;
  manifestYaml: string;
}
```

The `skills:resolve` hook handler is in `packages/skills/src/plugin.ts:424-446`. It calls `store.resolve(input.skillIds)` (global) and optionally `userStore.resolve(ownerUserId, skillIds)` (user-wins merge). The store's `resolve()` method (`packages/skills/src/store.ts:143-160`) fetches DB rows and calls `rowToGlobalResolved(row)` for each. `rowToGlobalResolved` (`packages/skills/src/_row-mappers.ts:82-89`) re-parses the manifest yaml via `parseCapabilities(row.manifest_yaml, row.skill_id)` — i.e., `parseSkillManifest()` is called fresh on every resolve. The full `SkillCapabilities` (including `allowedHosts`, `credentials`, `mcpServers`) flows from stored YAML → ParsedManifest.capabilities → ResolvedSkill.capabilities.

---

### 5. Row mappers

`packages/skills/src/_row-mappers.ts`. Key functions:
- `parseCapabilities(manifestYaml, skillId)` (line 29): calls `parseSkillManifest`, logs corrupt manifest to stderr and returns `EMPTY_CAPABILITIES` on failure
- `rowToGlobalResolved(row)` (line 82): returns `{ id, capabilities: parseCapabilities(...), bodyMd, manifestYaml }`
- `rowToUserResolved(row)` (line 123): same shape, used for user-scoped rows
- `rowToGlobalSummary` / `rowToGlobalDetail` / `rowToUserSummary` / `rowToUserDetail`: also call `parseCapabilities`

`BaseSkillRow` shape (line 46): `skill_id`, `description`, `manifest_yaml`, `body_md`, `version`, `default_attached`, `source_url`, `updated_at`.

---

### 6. Orchestrator allowlist union

`packages/chat-orchestrator/src/orchestrator.ts`:

**Building `unionedAllowlist`** (lines 960-1005):
```
baseAllowSet = new Set(['api.anthropic.com'])  // or agent.allowedHosts
for attachment of attachments:
  for host of skill.capabilities.allowedHosts:
    baseAllowSet.add(host)
unionedAllowlist = [...baseAllowSet]   // line 1005
```
This is `skill.capabilities.allowedHosts` — the already-unioned field from `parseSkillManifest` that includes per-mcpServer hosts and url hosts.

**Sandbox open-session payload field names:**
- Allowed hosts → passed to `proxy:open-session` as **`allowlist`** (line 1055): `{ allowlist: unionedAllowlist, credentials: unionedCreds }`
- MCP servers → passed to `sandbox:open-session` as **`installedSkills[].mcpServers`** (lines 1037-1044, 1165):
  ```ts
  const installedSkillsForSandbox: InstalledSkillForSandbox[] = unionedSkills.map(s => ({
    id: s.id,
    skillMd: '---\n' + s.manifestYaml + ... + s.bodyMd,
    mcpServers: s.capabilities.mcpServers ?? [],
  }));
  ```
  `InstalledSkillForSandbox` (line 334): `{ id, skillMd, mcpServers: McpServerSpecForOrch[] }`. No cross-skill union — each skill keeps its own group.

---

### 7. Dockerfile

`container/agent/Dockerfile`:

- **Base image** (both stages): `node:20-bookworm-slim` (lines 55, 94)
- **Non-root user**: `axagent`, UID/GID 1000:1000, shell `/usr/sbin/nologin` (lines 123-127). The base `node` user is deleted first.
- **Runtime apt installs** (lines 101-103): `ca-certificates tini git git-lfs` — **no python3, no pip, no uv/uvx**.
- **npx/node**: present via the Node 20 base image. No explicit npm/npx install needed.
- **python3**: NOT present.
- **uv/uvx**: NOT present.

For the design's additions, the natural insertion point is after line 103 (the existing apt block), either as a second `COPY --from=ghcr.io/astral-sh/uv:... /uv /uvx /usr/local/bin/` line (before the non-root user creation so perms land right) and an `apt-get install python3` added to the existing `apt-get install` command on line 102.

---

### 8. Error codes

All `ManifestCode` values are defined as a union type at `packages/skills-parser/src/manifest.ts:4-17`:
```ts
export type ManifestCode =
  | 'invalid-yaml' | 'invalid-manifest' | 'inline-secret-forbidden'
  | 'invalid-name' | 'invalid-description' | 'invalid-version'
  | 'capability-deferred' | 'invalid-host' | 'invalid-slot'
  | 'duplicate-slot' | 'invalid-kind' | 'invalid-mcp-command'
  | 'invalid-mcp-transport';
```
`invalid-mcp-command` and `invalid-mcp-transport` (Phase B) are already present. The design's "packages.go rejected" and "malformed name rejected" cases will need new codes — no `invalid-package` or `invalid-package-name` code exists yet.

---

### 9. Test files

**Manifest/capabilities parser tests** (`@ax/skills-parser`):
- `packages/skills-parser/src/__tests__/manifest.test.ts` — unit tests for `parseSkillManifest`, vitest, inline YAML string fixtures (`SAMPLE_OK`), pattern: `parseSkillManifest(yamlString)` + `expect(r.ok/r.code/r.value.*)`
- `packages/skills-parser/src/__tests__/manifest-mcp-servers.test.ts` — dedicated mcpServers tests
- `packages/skills-parser/src/__tests__/build.test.ts` — build/export checks

**Skills store / plugin tests** (`@ax/skills`):
- `packages/skills/src/__tests__/store.test.ts`

content.text:
I now have complete information for all 9 questions. Here is the report:

---

## Findings

### 1. Manifest/capabilities parser location

**BOTH packages exist today.** The design doc §5.8 is correct AND project memory is partially correct — they describe different layers:

- `@ax/skills-parser` (`packages/skills-parser/`) is the **actual parser package**. It contains `src/manifest.ts` (the `parseSkillManifest` function) and `src/capabilities.ts` (the type definitions). It has no dependencies on zod, only `js-yaml`.
- `@ax/skills` (`packages/skills/`) depends on `@ax/skills-parser` as a workspace dep. Its `packages/skills/src/manifest.ts` is a **one-line re-export**: `export { parseSkillManifest } from '@ax/skills-parser';`

The authoritative parser lives at:
- `/packages/skills-parser/src/manifest.ts` (`parseSkillManifest`)
- `/packages/skills-parser/src/capabilities.ts` (type definitions: `SkillCapabilities`, `McpServerSpec`, `CapabilitySlot`)

The `@ax/skills/src/manifest.ts` path referenced by project memory is a shim, not the implementation.

---

### 2. Capabilities grammar

**Validation: hand-rolled, no zod.** The parser is pure TypeScript with js-yaml. There is no zod in `@ax/skills-parser`.

**`SkillCapabilities` shape** (`packages/skills-parser/src/capabilities.ts:1-22`):
```ts
interface CapabilitySlot { slot: string; kind: 'api-key'; description?: string; }
interface McpServerSpec {
  name: string; transport: 'stdio' | 'http';
  command?: string; args?: string[]; env?: Record<string, string>; url?: string;
  allowedHosts: string[];      // union of per-server + url host
  credentials: CapabilitySlot[];
}
interface SkillCapabilities {
  allowedHosts: string[]; credentials: CapabilitySlot[]; mcpServers: McpServerSpec[];
}
```

**Key caps and validations** (`packages/skills-parser/src/manifest.ts`):
- `MCP_SERVERS_MAX = 8` (line 84), `MCP_ARGS_MAX = 32` (line 85), `MCP_ARG_LEN_MAX = 256` (line 86)
- `MCP_ENV_MAX = 32` (line 90), `MCP_ENV_LEN_MAX = 256` (line 91)
- Description max 240 chars (line 438)
- Inline-secret scan: `findSecretKey()` (line 41) walks all YAML nodes looking for keys in `SECRET_KEYS = new Set(['apiKey', 'token', 'password', 'secret'])` (line 31) — cyclic-safe via WeakSet
- Hosts: no wildcard, no scheme, no path, no IPv4, must match `HOSTNAME_RE` (two-label minimum)
- Slot names: `SLOT_RE = /^[A-Z][A-Z0-9_]{0,63}$/` (line 70); only `kind: 'api-key'` accepted; duplicate slots rejected with `duplicate-slot`
- Per-server hosts accumulated into parent `allowedHosts` set (the proxy sees the union)

---

### 3. MCP_COMMAND_ALLOW

Defined at `packages/skills-parser/src/manifest.ts:80`:
```ts
const MCP_COMMAND_ALLOW = new Set(['npx', 'node', 'bun', 'uvx', 'python', 'python3']);
```
Applied at line 262: any `stdio` command not in this set returns `{ ok: false, code: 'invalid-mcp-command', ... }`. Note: `uvx` and `python3` are already present — the design's proposed additions are already in the allowlist.

---

### 4. ResolvedSkill.capabilities and skills:resolve

`ResolvedSkill` is defined at `packages/skills/src/types.ts:31-36`:
```ts
export interface ResolvedSkill {
  id: string;
  capabilities: SkillCapabilities;   // imported from @ax/skills-parser
  bodyMd: string;
  manifestYaml: string;
}
```

The `skills:resolve` hook handler is in `packages/skills/src/plugin.ts:424-446`. It calls `store.resolve(input.skillIds)` (global) and optionally `userStore.resolve(ownerUserId, skillIds)` (user-wins merge). The store's `resolve()` method (`packages/skills/src/store.ts:143-160`) fetches DB rows and calls `rowToGlobalResolved(row)` for each. `rowToGlobalResolved` (`packages/skills/src/_row-mappers.ts:82-89`) re-parses the manifest yaml via `parseCapabilities(row.manifest_yaml, row.skill_id)` — i.e., `parseSkillManifest()` is called fresh on every resolve. The full `SkillCapabilities` (including `allowedHosts`, `credentials`, `mcpServers`) flows from stored YAML → ParsedManifest.capabilities → ResolvedSkill.capabilities.

---

### 5. Row mappers

`packages/skills/src/_row-mappers.ts`. Key functions:
- `parseCapabilities(manifestYaml, skillId)` (line 29): calls `parseSkillManifest`, logs corrupt manifest to stderr and returns `EMPTY_CAPABILITIES` on failure
- `rowToGlobalResolved(row)` (line 82): returns `{ id, capabilities: parseCapabilities(...), bodyMd, manifestYaml }`
- `rowToUserResolved(row)` (line 123): same shape, used for user-scoped rows
- `rowToGlobalSummary` / `rowToGlobalDetail` / `rowToUserSummary` / `rowToUserDetail`: also call `parseCapabilities`

`BaseSkillRow` shape (line 46): `skill_id`, `description`, `manifest_yaml`, `body_md`, `version`, `default_attached`, `source_url`, `updated_at`.

---

### 6. Orchestrator allowlist union

`packages/chat-orchestrator/src/orchestrator.ts`:

**Building `unionedAllowlist`** (lines 960-1005):
```
baseAllowSet = new Set(['api.anthropic.com'])  // or agent.allowedHosts
for attachment of attachments:
  for host of skill.capabilities.allowedHosts:
    baseAllowSet.add(host)
unionedAllowlist = [...baseAllowSet]   // line 1005
```
This is `skill.capabilities.allowedHosts` — the already-unioned field from `parseSkillManifest` that includes per-mcpServer hosts and url hosts.

**Sandbox open-session payload field names:**
- Allowed hosts → passed to `proxy:open-session` as **`allowlist`** (line 1055): `{ allowlist: unionedAllowlist, credentials: unionedCreds }`
- MCP servers → passed to `sandbox:open-session` as **`installedSkills[].mcpServers`** (lines 1037-1044, 1165):
  ```ts
  const installedSkillsForSandbox: InstalledSkillForSandbox[] = unionedSkills.map(s => ({
    id: s.id,
    skillMd: '---\n' + s.manifestYaml + ... + s.bodyMd,
    mcpServers: s.capabilities.mcpServers ?? [],
  }));
  ```
  `InstalledSkillForSandbox` (line 334): `{ id, skillMd, mcpServers: McpServerSpecForOrch[] }`. No cross-skill union — each skill keeps its own group.

---

### 7. Dockerfile

`container/agent/Dockerfile`:

- **Base image** (both stages): `node:20-bookworm-slim` (lines 55, 94)
- **Non-root user**: `axagent`, UID/GID 1000:1000, shell `/usr/sbin/nologin` (lines 123-127). The base `node` user is deleted first.
- **Runtime apt installs** (lines 101-103): `ca-certificates tini git git-lfs` — **no python3, no pip, no uv/uvx**.
- **npx/node**: present via the Node 20 base image. No explicit npm/npx install needed.
- **python3**: NOT present.
- **uv/uvx**: NOT present.

For the design's additions, the natural insertion point is after line 103 (the existing apt block), either as a second `COPY --from=ghcr.io/astral-sh/uv:... /uv /uvx /usr/local/bin/` line (before the non-root user creation so perms land right) and an `apt-get install python3` added to the existing `apt-get install` command on line 102.

---

### 8. Error codes

All `ManifestCode` values are defined as a union type at `packages/skills-parser/src/manifest.ts:4-17`:
```ts
export type ManifestCode =
  | 'invalid-yaml' | 'invalid-manifest' | 'inline-secret-forbidden'
  | 'invalid-name' | 'invalid-description' | 'invalid-version'
  | 'capability-deferred' | 'invalid-host' | 'invalid-slot'
  | 'duplicate-slot' | 'invalid-kind' | 'invalid-mcp-command'
  | 'invalid-mcp-transport';
```
`invalid-mcp-command` and `invalid-mcp-transport` (Phase B) are already present. The design's "packages.go rejected" and "malformed name rejected" cases will need new codes — no `invalid-package` or `invalid-package-name` code exists yet.

---

### 9. Test files

**Manifest/capabilities parser tests** (`@ax/skills-parser`):
- `packages/skills-parser/src/__tests__/manifest.test.ts` — unit tests for `parseSkillManifest`, vitest, inline YAML string fixtures (`SAMPLE_OK`), pattern: `parseSkillManifest(yamlString)` + `expect(r.ok/r.code/r.value.*)`
- `packages/skills-parser/src/__tests__/manifest-mcp-servers.test.ts` — dedicated mcpServers tests
- `packages/skills-parser/src/__tests__/build.test.ts` — build/export checks

**Skills store / plugin tests** (`@ax/skills`):
- `packages/skills/src/__tests__/store.test.ts`
- `packages/skills/src/__tests__/plugin.test.ts`

**Orchestrator allowlist-union tests** (`@ax/chat-orchestrator`):
- `packages/chat-orchestrator/src/__tests__/orchestrator.test.ts` — vitest, uses `buildSkillsHooks()` helper (line 1564) that registers a fake `skills:resolve` service returning inline `ResolvedSkill` fixtures; the `buildProxyHooks()` helper captures `lastOpenInput`; allowlist assertions at line 1671-1672; mcpServers threading tested at line 1747 ("unions skill-bundled mcpServers into the sandbox open-session input").

---

### Design doc claims that do NOT match reality

1. **§5.8 path discrepancy**: Both `packages/skills-parser/src/manifest.ts` AND `packages/skills/src/manifest.ts` exist, but only the former is the real implementation. `@ax/skills/src/manifest.ts` is a one-line re-export. Project memory ("parser is at `packages/skills/src/manifest.ts`") is technically true but misleading — the actual code is in `@ax/skills-parser`.

2. **`MCP_COMMAND_ALLOW` already includes `uvx` and `python3`** (line 80) — the design implies these need to be added, but they are already there.

3. **No `invalid-package` or `invalid-package-name` error code exists** — the design's "packages.go rejected with clear message" + "malformed name rejected" require new `ManifestCode` entries.

4. **Dockerfile has no python3/uv** — confirmed, both need to be added.

Both Explore agents returned grounded findings. Reconciling the design against reality surfaced four material deviations worth recording (the "check plan vs reality" discipline):

1. **B / YAGNI cut:** `GIT_TERMINAL_PROMPT=0` is *already* stamped in both `sandbox-k8s/pod-spec.ts` and `sandbox-subprocess/open-session.ts`. Design §4.3/§4.6 asks to add it — already done. → cut to a regression assertion, don't re-stamp.
2. **B / framing complexity:** the MITM path is pure per-chunk streaming today (`registry.replaceAllBuffer` on each `clientTls 'data'`); the design's "buffer the head" only describes ONE head, but git smart-HTTP reuses the CONNECT tunnel for GET `/info/refs` *then* POST `/git-upload-pack`, both carrying `Authorization`. Single-head buffering would corrupt the POST's auth. Needs a per-request re-arming state machine.
3. **B / canary:** scanning is inline `chunk.includes(token)`, not a registry function — the decoded Basic value must be canary-scanned inside the transform (design §4.5 anticipates this).
4. **D / path confirmed:** parser lives in `@ax/skills-parser` (design §5.8 is right; memory's `@ax/skills/src/manifest.ts` is a re-export shim). `MCP_COMMAND_ALLOW` already has `uvx`/`python3` (irrelevant to `packages` but worth noting).

.claude/memory/decisions.md
--- tail of worktree decisions.md ---
| 2026-05-21 | Title bug: replace the one-shot `assistantTurnCount !== 1` gate with a **bounded retry** (`<= MAX_TITLE_ATTEMPT_TURNS`, =3) while untitled | Root cause of "inconsistent" titles is the single attempt: if it's skipped/fails for ANY transient reason (jsonl-read race in `conversations:get`, llm:call error, validation rejection) the chat is permanently untitled. `title !== null` guard + `ifNull:true` on set-title already make re-attempts idempotent, so retrying on the next few assistant turns is safe; the cap bounds title-LLM spend if a model keeps emitting unusable titles. | Keep one-shot + only fix the UI (rejected: leaves "never generated" class unfixed); unbounded retry (rejected: unbounded LLM spend on degenerate output) |
| 2026-05-21 | Title bug (UI half): widen `generateTitle` poll window (more attempts + backoff, ~12-15s) instead of a client-side first-message fallback title | The 3×1s poll often gives up before the backend round-trip (jsonl read + haiku) lands, permanently caching "New Chat" until a full reload. Widening catches the common case. A client fallback title (first-message snippet) was rejected: it would later flip to the LLM title on reload — jarring. Residual (title landing after the widened window still needs a list() refresh) tracked as TODO. | Client-side fallback title (rejected: diverges from backend, flips on reload); push/websocket title update (rejected: out of scope for MVP) |
| 2026-05-21 | Attachment chip bug: **reconstruct the `attachment` block from the stored text-mention on the host read path** (`conversations:get`), gated to the conversation's own `.ax/uploads/<conversationId>/` prefix | Under runner-owned-sessions the jsonl stores the runner's *translation* (text-mention `User attached '<name>' at <path> (<mime>)`), not the original `attachment` block — so the original is genuinely gone. The mention embeds path+name+mime (everything the chip needs; `sizeBytes` is dropped by the render path anyway). Reconstruction needs no schema/table and **retroactively fixes already-broken chats** (no migration). Format owned by a shared `formatAttachmentMention`/`parseAttachmentMention` pair in `@ax/ipc-protocol` so producer (runner) and consumer (conversations) can't drift. Path-prefix gate + `AttachmentBlockSchema.safeParse` + role==='user'-only keep untrusted jsonl from injecting an out-of-scope downloadable ref. | Host-side per-turn attachment-block table overlaid on read (rejected: new table+hook+turnId-matching, heavier, and needs a migration to fix old chats; `sizeBytes` is the only extra fidelity and it's unused by the render path); client-side parse in blocksToParts (rejected: leaves the wire shape lossy for all other consumers of conversations:get) |

Single-head buffering would transform the GET but leave the POST's base64 placeholder corrupted → fetch fails after refs succeed. State machine: buffer head→transform Basic→`Content-Length`-counted body then re-arm to head; `Transfer-Encoding: chunked` or oversized head → flush + verbatim passthrough, no re-arm (the chunked POST is the clone's last request, so not re-arming after it is safe). Header-only rewrite; bodies keep existing verbatim per-chunk sub (honors I1). | Single head per connection (rejected: corrupts the POST auth on keep-alive); full chunked de-framing to re-arm after chunked bodies (rejected: YAGNI — git's chunked POST is terminal; adds a chunk-size parser for no MVP gain); line-scan for `Authorization:` anywhere in the stream (rejected: false-matches inside POST bodies could corrupt them) |
| 2026-05-22 | B canary parity: run the canary scan on the **decoded** `user:pass` inside the transform, before re-encode | Canary scanning today is inline `chunk.includes(token)` on raw decrypted bytes (`listener.ts`), not a registry function — a base64-wrapped canary in a Basic blob would slip past it once we start decoding. Design §4.5 mandates parity. Extract a small `scanForCanary(text, tokens)` helper so head-transform and the existing per-chunk path share it. | Leave canary scan only on raw bytes (rejected: the new decode step blinds it — a real exfil gap) |
| 2026-05-22 | Sub-project D targets **`@ax/skills-parser`** (`src/manifest.ts` + `src/capabilities.ts`), not `@ax/skills` | Explore confirmed `@ax/skills/src/manifest.ts` is a one-line re-export of `@ax/skills-parser`'s `parseSkillManifest`; the real grammar + hand-rolled validation + `ManifestCode` union live in `@ax/skills-parser`. Design §5.8 path is correct; project-memory note ("parser at packages/skills") is the shim. `packages` grammar, caps, and new error codes go in skills-parser; `EMPTY_CAPABILITIES` + `SkillCapabilities` type gain `packages`; resolve carries it automatically (re-parse on every `skills:resolve`). | Edit the `@ax/skills` shim (rejected: not the implementation) |
| 2026-05-22 | D rejects unsupported ecosystems (`packages.go`) and malformed names with **new `ManifestCode` entries** `invalid-package` (bad name shape / caps) + `unsupported-package-ecosystem` (go and any non-npm/pypi key) | Mirrors the existing per-feature code pattern (`invalid-mcp-command`, `invalid-mcp-transport`); a clear distinct code lets the admin UI message precisely ("Go isn't supported yet" vs "package name invalid"). Grammar shaped so adding `go` later is just removing it from the unsupported set + a registry-host union. | One generic `invalid-package` for both (rejected: can't distinguish "not yet supported" from "malformed"); silently drop unknown ecosystems (rejected: I4/invariant — untrusted manifest content must fail loudly, not be ignored) |
| 2026-05-22 | uv/uvx added to the agent image via `COPY --from=ghcr.io/astral-sh/uv:<pinned-tag> /uv /uvx /usr/local/bin/` with a **version tag** (digest-pin deferred); `python3` from Debian bookworm via the existing apt line | Matches design §5.5/§5.7 and the repo's existing ca-certificates/tini "pin by version now, digest-pin as a tracked follow-up" posture. uv is a static Rust binary (lightweight, no runtime deps); reusing system `python3` means `uvx` never downloads a Python build (no extra egress host). The implementing subagent picks the latest stable uv tag and the image smoke test (`uv`, `uvx`, `python3` present + executable as `axagent`) verifies it. | pip+venv management by us (rejected: design explicitly avoids — uvx is ephemeral-env on demand); download python-build-standalone (rejected: extra egress host, system python3 suffices for MVP); digest-pin now (rejected: follow-up alongside the existing tini/ca-certs pin TODO) |

content.content:
20	| 2026-05-21 | Title bug: replace the one-shot `assistantTurnCount !== 1` gate with a **bounded retry** (`<= MAX_TITLE_ATTEMPT_TURNS`, =3) while untitled | Root cause of "inconsistent" titles is the single attempt: if it's skipped/fails for ANY transient reason (jsonl-read race in `conversations:get`, llm:call error, validation rejection) the chat is permanently untitled. `title !== null` guard + `ifNull:true` on set-title already make re-attempts idempotent, so retrying on the next few assistant turns is safe; the cap bounds title-LLM spend if a model keeps emitting unusable titles. | Keep one-shot + only fix the UI (rejected: leaves "never generated" class unfixed); unbounded retry (rejected: unbounded LLM spend on degenerate output) |
21	| 2026-05-21 | Title bug (UI half): widen `generateTitle` poll window (more attempts + backoff, ~12-15s) instead of a client-side first-message fallback title | The 3×1s poll often gives up before the backend round-trip (jsonl read + haiku) lands, permanently caching "New Chat" until a full reload. Widening catches the common case. A client fallback title (first-message snippet) was rejected: it would later flip to the LLM title on reload — jarring. Residual (title landing after the widened window still needs a list() refresh) tracked as TODO. | Client-side fallback title (rejected: diverges from backend, flips on reload); push/websocket title update (rejected: out of scope for MVP) |
22	| 2026-05-21 | Attachment chip bug: **reconstruct the `attachment` block from the stored text-mention on the host read path** (`conversations:get`), gated to the conversation's own `.ax/uploads/<conversationId>/` prefix | Under runner-owned-sessions the jsonl stores the runner's *translation* (text-mention `User attached '<name>' at <path> (<mime>)`), not the original `attachment` block — so the original is genuinely gone. The mention embeds path+name+mime (everything the chip needs; `sizeBytes` is dropped by the render path anyway). Reconstruction needs no schema/table and **retroactively fixes already-broken chats** (no migration). Format owned by a shared `formatAttachmentMention`/`parseAttachmentMention` pair in `@ax/ipc-protocol` so producer (runner) and consumer (conversations) can't drift. Path-prefix gate + `AttachmentBlockSchema.safeParse` + role==='user'-only keep untrusted jsonl from injecting an out-of-scope downloadable ref. | Host-side per-turn attachment-block table overlaid on read (rejected: new table+hook+turnId-matching, heavier, and needs a migration to fix old chats; `sizeBytes` is the only extra fidelity and it's unused by the render path); client-side parse in blocksToParts (rejected: leaves the wire shape lossy for all other consumers of conversations:get) |
23

+| 2026-05-22 | Sub-project B Basic-auth transform uses a **per-request re-arming HTTP/1.1 framing state machine** in the MITM client→upstream path, not single-head-per-connection as design §4.2 literally describes | git smart-HTTP reuses ONE CONNECT tunnel for `GET /info/refs?service=git-upload-pack` (no body) **then** `POST /git-upload-pack` (body), and with creds in the URL libcurl sends `Authorization: Basic` **preemptively on both**. Single-head buffering would transform the GET but leave the POST's base64 placeholder corrupted → fetch fails after refs succeed. State machine: buffer head→transform Basic→`Content-Length`-counted body then re-arm to head; `Transfer-Encoding: chunked` or oversized head → flush + verbatim passthrough, no re-arm (the chunked POST is the clone's last request, so not re-arming after it is safe). Header-only rewrite; bodies keep existing verbatim per-chunk sub (honors I1). | Single head per connection (rejected: corrupts the POST auth on keep-alive); full chunked de-framing to re-arm after chunked bodies (rejected: YAGNI — git's chunked POST is terminal; adds a chunk-size parser for no MVP gain); line-scan for `Authorization:` anywhere in the stream (rejected: false-matches inside POST bodies could corrupt them) |

+| 2026-05-22 | B canary parity: run the canary scan on the **decoded** `user:pass` inside the transform, before re-encode | Canary scanning today is inline `chunk.includes(token)` on raw decrypted bytes (`listener.ts`), not a registry function — a base64-wrapped canary in a Basic blob would slip past it once we start decoding. Design §4.5 mandates parity. Extract a small `scanForCanary(text, tokens)` helper so head-transform and the existing per-chunk path share it. | Leave canary scan only on raw bytes (rejected: the new decode step blinds it — a real exfil gap) |

+| 2026-05-22 | Sub-project D targets **`@ax/skills-parser`** (`src/manifest.ts` + `src/capabilities.ts`), not `@ax/skills` | Explore confirmed `@ax/skills/src/manifest.ts` is a one-line re-export of `@ax/skills-parser`'s `parseSkillManifest`; the real grammar + hand-rolled validation + `ManifestCode` union live in `@ax/skills-parser`. Design §5.8 path is correct; project-memory note ("parser at packages/skills") is the shim. `packages` grammar, caps, and new error codes go in skills-parser; `EMPTY_CAPABILITIES` + `SkillCapabilities` type gain `packages`; resolve carries it automatically (re-parse on every `skills:resolve`). | Edit the `@ax/skills` shim (rejected: not the implementation) |

+| 2026-05-22 | D rejects unsupported ecosystems (`packages.go`) and malformed names with **new `ManifestCode` entries** `invalid-package` (bad name shape / caps) + `unsupported-package-ecosystem` (go and any non-npm/pypi key) | Mirrors the existing per-feature code pattern (`invalid-mcp-command`, `invalid-mcp-transport`); a clear distinct code lets the admin UI message precisely ("Go isn't supported yet" vs "package name invalid"). Grammar shaped so adding `go` later is just removing it from the unsupported set + a registry-host union. | One generic `invalid-package` for both (rejected: can't distinguish "not yet supported" from "malformed"); silently drop unknown ecosystems (rejected: I4/invariant — untrusted manifest content must fail loudly, not be ignored) |

+| 2026-05-22 | uv/uvx added to the agent image via `COPY --from=ghcr.io/astral-sh/uv:<pinned-tag> /uv /uvx /usr/local/bin/` with a **version tag** (digest-pin deferred); `python3` from Debian bookworm via the existing apt line | Matches design §5.5/§5.7 and the repo's existing ca-certificates/tini "pin by version now, digest-pin as a tracked follow-up" posture. uv is a static Rust binary (lightweight, no runtime deps); reusing system `python3` means `uvx` never downloads a Python build (no extra egress host). The implementing subagent picks the latest stable uv tag and the image smoke test (`uv`, `uvx`, `python3` present + executable as `axagent`) verifies it. | pip+venv management by us (rejected: design explicitly avoids — uvx is ephemeral-env on demand); download python-build-standalone (rejected: extra egress host, system python3 suffices for MVP); digest-pin now (rejected: follow-up alongside the existing tini/ca-certs pin TODO) |

content.text:
Base directory for this skill: /Users/vpulim/.claude/plugins/cache/claude-plugins-official/superpowers/5.1.0/skills/writing-plans

# Writing Plans

## Overview

Write comprehensive implementation plans assuming the engineer has zero context for our codebase and questionable taste. Document everything they need to know: which files to touch for each task, code, testing, docs they might need to check, how to test it. Give them the whole plan as bite-sized tasks. DRY. YAGNI. TDD. Frequent commits.

Assume they are a skilled developer, but know almost nothing about our toolset or problem domain. Assume they don't know good test design very well.

**Announce at start:** "I'm using the writing-plans skill to create the implementation plan."

**Context:** If working in an isolated worktree, it should have been created via the `superpowers:using-git-worktrees` skill at execution time.

**Save plans to:** `docs/superpowers/plans/YYYY-MM-DD-<feature-name>.md`
- (User preferences for plan location override this default)

## Scope Check

If the spec covers multiple independent subsystems, it should have been broken into sub-project specs during brainstorming. If it wasn't, suggest breaking this into separate plans — one per subsystem. Each plan should produce working, testable software on its own.

## File Structure

Before defining tasks, map out which files will be created or modified and what each one is responsible for. This is where decomposition decisions get locked in.

- Design units with clear boundaries and well-defined interfaces. Each file should have one clear responsibility.
- You reason best about code you can hold in context at once, and your edits are more reliable when files are focused. Prefer smaller, focused files over large ones that do too much.
- Files that change together should live together. Split by responsibility, not by technical layer.
- In existing codebases, follow established patterns. If the codebase uses large files, don't unilaterally restructure - but if a file you're modifying has grown unwieldy, including a split in the plan is reasonable.

This structure informs the task decomposition. Each task should produce self-contained changes that make sense independently.

## Bite-Sized Task Granularity

**Each step is one action (2-5 minutes):**
- "Write the failing test" - step
- "Run it to make sure it fails" - step
- "Implement the minimal code to make the test pass" - step
- "Run the tests and make sure they pass" - step
- "Commit" - step

## Plan Document Header

**Every plan MUST start with this header:**

```markdown
# [Feature Name] Implementation Plan

> **For agentic workers:** REQUIRED SUB-SKILL: Use superpowers:subagent-driven-development (recommended) or superpowers:executing-plans to implement this plan task-by-task. Steps use checkbox (`- [ ]`) syntax for tracking.

**Goal:** [One sentence describing what this builds]

**Architecture:** [2-3 sentences about approach]

**Tech Stack:** [Key technologies/libraries]

---
```

## Task Structure

````markdown
### Task N: [Component Name]

**Files:**
- Create: `exact/path/to/file.py`
- Modify: `exact/path/to/existing.py:123-145`
- Test: `tests/exact/path/to/test.py`

- [ ] **Step 1: Write the failing test**

```python
def test_specific_behavior():
    result = function(input)
    assert result == expected
```

- [ ] **Step 2: Run test to verify it fails**

Run: `pytest tests/path/test.py::test_name -v`
Expected: FAIL with "function not defined"

- [ ] **Step 3: Write minimal implementation**

```python
def function(input):
    return expected
```

- [ ] **Step 4: Run test to verify it passes**

Run: `pytest tests/path/test.py::test_name -v`
Expected: PASS

- [ ] **Step 5: Commit**

```bash
git add tests/path/test.py src/path/file.py
git commit -m "feat: add specific feature"
```
````

## No Placeholders

Every step must contain the actual content an engineer needs. These are **plan failures** — never write them:
- "TBD", "TODO", "implement later", "fill in details"
- "Add appropriate error handling" / "add validation" / "handle edge cases"
- "Write tests for the above" (without actual test code)
- "Similar to Task N" (repeat the code — the engineer may be reading tasks out of order)
- Steps that describe what to do without showing how (code blocks required for code steps)
- References to types, functions, or methods not defined in any task

## Remember
- Exact file paths always
- Complete code in every step — if a step changes code, show the code
- Exact commands with expected output
- DRY, YAGNI, TDD, frequent commits

## Self-Review

After writing the complete plan, look at the spec with fresh eyes and check the plan against it. This is a checklist you run yourself — not a subagent dispatch.

**1. Spec coverage:** Skim each section/requirement in the spec. Can you point to a task that implements it? List any gaps.

**2. Placeholder scan:** Search your plan for red flags — any of the patterns from the "No Placeholders" section above. Fix them.

**3. Type consistency:** Do the types, method signatures, and property names you used in later tasks match what you defined in earlier tasks? A function called `clearLayers()` in Task 3 but `clearFullLayers()` in Task 7 is a bug.

If you find issues, fix them inline. No need to re-review — just fix and move on. If you find a spec requirement with no task, add the task.

## Execution Handoff

After saving the plan, offer execution choice:

**"Plan complete and saved to `docs/superpowers/plans/<filename>.md`. Two execution options:**

**1. Subagent-Driven (recommended)** - I dispatch a fresh subagent per task, review between tasks, fast iteration

**2. Inline Execution** - Execute tasks in this session using executing-plans, batch execution with checkpoints

**Which approach?"**

**If Subagent-Driven chosen:**
- **REQUIRED SUB-SKILL:** Use superpowers:subagent-driven-development
- Fresh subagent per task + two-stage review

**If Inline Execution chosen:**
- **REQUIRED SUB-SKILL:** Use superpowers:executing-plans
- Batch execution with checkpoints for review

I'm using the writing-plans skill to create the implementation plan. I have the design fully reconciled against current code. Let me write the plan to the repo's convention path (`docs/plans/`, overriding the skill default).

content.text:
Task B3 complete.

## Report

**Commit:** `6d6bd842` — `feat(credential-proxy): wire RequestFramer into MITM path for git Basic-auth (B)` (one commit, no amends; `.claude/memory/decisions.md` left unstaged as pre-existing).

**Full-package test:** `pnpm --filter @ax/credential-proxy test` → **93 passed (12 files)**, up from a 90-test baseline (+2 new MITM B3 cases, +1 new framer regression test).

**Build:** `pnpm --filter @ax/credential-proxy build` → **tsc clean**. Lint clean on the four changed files.

**Harness reuse:** Reused the suite's real helpers — `mintCA`, `generateDomainCert`, `SharedCredentialRegistry`/`CredentialPlaceholderMap`, and `startProxyListener` — and added two small helpers in the same file: `startMultiCapturingUpstream` (the existing single-request `startCapturingUpstream` only captures one head, so this one loops over pipelined requests on one keep-alive socket to record both Authorization headers) and `openMitmTunnel` (raw `net.connect` → CONNECT → inner `tls.connect` trusting the test CA), which lets the test write hand-crafted git-clone-shaped GET+POST heads over the established `clientTls` socket exactly as B3 describes.

**Wiring done per B3 Step 3:** one `RequestFramer(registry, canaryTokens, { onOversizedHead })` per connection; raw-chunk canary scan routed through `findCanaryHit`; decoded-Basic-blob canary and the raw hit both route through a shared `blockCanary` local closure (identical 403/clientTls.end/targetTls.destroy/audit path — no duplication); `targetTls.write` only when `out.length > 0`. The pre-TLS `head`-param flush (~lines 553–557) and all egress/destroy semantics are untouched. No decoded values logged. No `.exec(` introduced.

**Deviation (noted):** The integration exposed a real bug in the committed B2 framer — `RequestFramer.process()` pushed the head with only the Basic-decode transform applied, dropping the verbatim `replaceAllBuffer` the pre-framer code ran over the whole chunk. That broke every existing MITM test carrying a `Authorization: Bearer ax-cred:…` placeholder in the head (6 failures across acceptance/egress/plugin/integration/mitm suites). Fix: the framer now runs the Basic-transformed head through `replacer.replaceAllBuffer` too (the Basic line already holds the re-encoded real value, so no double-substitution). This required touching `request-framer.ts` and adding a Bearer-in-head regression unit test — beyond B3's stated "listener-only" file list, but necessary to make the wiring correct and to keep B2's framer sound in isolation. I folded these two files into the single B3 commit rather than amending the B2 commit (per the no-amend constraint). All prior tests are green again.
